# The New Google Stitch - Are Designers Finished?

**Channel**: DesignCourse
**Duration**: 10:18
**Language**: English (auto-generated)
**URL**: https://youtube.com/watch?v=qwEKpbEjUHY
**Transcribed**: 2026-03-23 03:09

---

## Organized Notes

**What is Google Stitch?**

- Google's revamped **"vibe design" tool** at stitch.withgoogle.com
- Two modes: **web** or **app** design
- Model selection available (uses **Gemini 3.1 Pro** for best results)
- Can accept **PRD files** as input to generate designs
- Has preset **color schemes** (limited, not customizable) or "let Stitch decide"
- Generation is **fast** — roughly a minute per design

**First Impressions (Honest Designer Feedback)**

- Output is **"still mid"** — a top-tier designer would produce something significantly better
- No major leap or transformation from the previous version
- Color schemes are **bland and desaturated** — nothing sticks out
- Makes poor design decisions: e.g., watermark-style sample property images that "look like crap"
- At best, useful as a **prototyping/ideation tool**, not for polished final output

**Editing Experience — Major Frustrations**

- No **visual builder** — can't click sections to modify them intuitively
- Only supports selecting **individual elements**, not section-level changes (e.g., can't change a section's background color)
- Text editing is **buggy** — clicking "edit text" then trying to type/delete is unreliable and frustrating
- Can't easily specify context-aware changes like "take this section and change its color"

**Iterative Prompting with Reference Images**

- Pasted a **Mobin.com screenshot** as a style reference with explicit feedback
- Stitch acknowledged the feedback well verbally ("atmospheric depth, large scale topography, glass morphism")
- But the **generated variations were worse** than the original — "far worse than even what it originally prompted me with"
- Best of 3 variations was still **"low tier"** — looks like work from a UI designer with a couple months of experience
- Glass morphism attempt had **contrast issues** (text hard to read)

**Key Takeaway: Designers Are NOT Finished**

- "Vibe design" tools are **only as good as the underlying models** (Gemini, Claude, OpenAI) — all tools share the same limitations
- Users without design experience are **"left to its discretion"** with no way to improve output quality
- **Design still matters** — "we're not anywhere near the era where design's dead"
- These tools need someone with **design literacy** to steer iterative prompting toward quality output
- Current state: useful for **quick prototyping and idea generation**, not production-ready design
